title: Market Brief — Monday, June 22, 2026
summary: GPU compute is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 36% for A100, 46% for H100, 53% for H200, and 44% for B200, all reflecting 17-to-25 percentage point utilization drops over 30 days, meaning supply is flooding in well above the oversupply threshold on none but trending that way quickly. The biggest daily move is A100 spot down 6.2% to $1.00/hr, with H200 close behind at minus 5.1%, while B200 bucks the trend at plus 2.0%.
paragraphs: GPU compute is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 36% for A100, 46% for H100, 53% for H200, and 44% for B200, all reflecting 17-to-25 percentage point utilization drops over 30 days, meaning supply is flooding in well above the oversupply threshold on none but trending that way quickly. The biggest daily move is A100 spot down 6.2% to $1.00/hr, with H200 close behind at minus 5.1%, while B200 bucks the trend at plus 2.0%.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), serving Llama 70B on H100 runs $8.30/M tokens, making every medium-tier provider selling around $3/M a cash furnace losing roughly $5.30 per million tokens. DeepSeek: Burning Cash, pricing V3.2 output at $0.34/M against $4.00/M TCO on B200. OpenAI gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M output is similarly underwater. Anthropic: Neutral, with Haiku 4.5 at $5/M holding margin. Google: Weak, Gemini 2.5 Flash at $2.50/M barely covers B200 TCO.

B200 is undervalued at $4.38/hr spot given it is the only GPU delivering positive margins at medium-tier token prices with just a 7% loss versus triple-digit losses on H100 and H200. o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued by at least 3x relative to Claude Opus 4.6 at $25/M for comparable frontier capability, so rotate premium inference spend from OpenAI to Anthropic and shift self-hosted workloads onto B200 before the spot floor hardens.
bullets: 36% util-avail on A100 marks the loosest legacy GPU supply since tracking began.
46% util-avail on H100 reflects a 24.8 percentage point utilization collapse in 30 days.
$0.18/M output for OpenAI gpt-oss-120b sits roughly $3.82 below TCO-adjusted B200 serving cost.
133.5% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest compute contract to hold.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$46 per DDR5 16Gb chip rose 1.32% week-over-week, the lone memory component ticking up amid broad GPU deflation.
